description: Length-matched V2 facts for safety invariants — off-target prediction, genotoxicity, toxicology markers.

facts.context: CRISPR off-target editing is predicted by:
facts.truth: seed region mismatch tolerance in PAM-proximal 10-12nt
distractors: random chromatin accessibility at unpredictable genomic loci
full 20nt exact complementarity with zero mismatch allowed
transgene copy number variation in the host cell genome

facts.context: Unbiased off-target detection methods like GUIDE-seq work by:
facts.truth: integrating tagged oligos at genome-wide DSB sites
distractors: amplifying only computationally predicted off-target loci
quantifying total guide RNA levels by fluorescent probes
measuring downstream protein fold changes via western blot

facts.context: High-fidelity Cas9 variants (eSpCas9, HiFi) reduce off-targets by:
facts.truth: weakening non-specific DNA contacts to enforce strict pairing
distractors: accelerating cleavage kinetics uniformly at all target sites
extending PAM recognition from NGG to a 6-base motif window
increasing nuclear localization signal copies on the protein

facts.context: Insertional mutagenesis from lentiviral vectors risks:
facts.truth: oncogene activation or tumor suppressor disruption at the site
distractors: transient episomal expression with no genomic integration event
selective cytotoxicity restricted to post-mitotic quiescent cells
uniform transgene silencing across every chromosomal insert site

facts.context: AAV vectors are mostly non-integrating, but residual integration:
facts.truth: occurs rarely and concentrates at double-strand break sites
distractors: reaches high frequency comparable to retroviral vector rates
targets exclusively prokaryotic genomes and not human cells
distributes uniformly across all chromosomal regions at random

facts.context: CRISPR double-strand breaks activate p53, causing:
facts.truth: cell cycle arrest and enrichment of p53-null clones
distractors: accelerated proliferation across the edited cell population
enhanced homology-directed repair efficiency at cut sites
immediate terminal differentiation of all transfected cells

facts.context: Cytokine release syndrome from gene therapy features:
facts.truth: elevated IL-6 and TNF-alpha driving systemic organ damage
distractors: decreased inflammatory markers with local injection reaction
selective IFN-beta rise without hemodynamic compromise noted
transient complement activation resolving before any symptoms

facts.context: High-dose AAV hepatotoxicity is monitored by:
distractors: serum creatinine and BUN levels for renal function screening
peripheral blood smear morphology detecting hemolytic anemia
cerebrospinal fluid protein for neuroinflammation surveillance

facts.context: AAV gene therapy thrombocytopenia is driven by:
facts.truth: complement activation and thrombotic microangiopathy at high dose
distractors: direct megakaryocyte suppression within bone marrow progenitor niche
autoimmune platelet destruction mediated by anti-GPIIb/IIIa antibodies
splenic sequestration from AAV capsid-induced splenomegaly at all dose

facts.context: AAV dorsal root ganglion toxicity occurs in:
facts.truth: high-dose systemic or intrathecal delivery causing sensory neuron loss
distractors: low-dose local injection restricted to specific viral capsid serotypes
oral administration routes affecting only peripheral motor neuron axons
all delivery methods equally regardless of vector dose or route chosen
